free_text: Actinide production in 136Xe bombardments of 249Cf
The Lawrence Berkeley Laboratory
SuperHILAC accelerator.
The isotopically pure 249Cf was deposited on the Be
backing foil as Cf203 in a 6 mm diam spot by the
molecular deposition method, and the amount of Cf was
measured by counting the 249Cf alpha particles in a
low solid angle counting apparatus.
Five Ge(Li) or high purity Ge gamma ray
detectors. Four 100 mm2 Si(Au) surface barrier
detectors.
Chemical fractions of the
individual actinide elements were prepared and the
gamma, alpha, and spontaneous fission activities of
the actinide isotopes were measured.
The uncertainties quoted represent 1 sigma
deviations and are meant to encompass uncertainties
due to counting statistics and uncertainties in the
detector efficiencies, the decay radiation abundances,
the chemical yield, and in the beam intensity during
irradiation.
None of the cross sections in this table have been
corrected for feeding from the decay of other
nuclides during the bombardment. One exception to
this is in the neutron-deficient Cm isotopes which
are fed by the alpha decay of Cf isotopes. The
feeding corrections in this case were so large that
the neutron-deficient Cm cross sections are not
presented in this work.
BP
